name: pycostaudit
description: Enterprise-grade cost tracking for Claude Code - advanced filtering, multi-org support, compliance, observability, and ML-powered optimization
author: Georgi Mammen Mullassery

tracking.fileFormatCosts: Tracks cost multipliers for file sources (CSV vs PDF URL = 36x)
tracking.operationTypeBreakdown: Isolates costs by operation type (browser vs file = 55x)
tracking.dataWarehouseQueries: Profiles database reads and S3 operations (100x-1000x+)
tracking.sessionTracking: Groups operations by context for root cause analysis

enterprise.advancedFiltering: 12 composable operators (EQ, NE, GT, GTE, LT, LTE, IN, NOT_IN, CONTAINS, BETWEEN, REGEX)
enterprise.multiOrg: Unlimited hierarchical departments with role-based access control
enterprise.compliance: SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI DSS, ISO 27001 audit logging
enterprise.observability: OpenTelemetry integration (Prometheus, Jaeger, Datadog, New Relic, OTLP)
enterprise.recommendations: 8 optimization strategies with ROI calculation and cost driver analysis

track_operation.description: Track a single LLM operation cost

operation_type.description: Type of operation that consumed tokens

start_session.description: Start a new cost tracking session

end_session.description: End a session and get cost summary

analyze_daily.description: Get today's cost breakdown by operation type, file format, and MCP

analyze_session.description: Analyze a session's costs with root cause diagnosis and recommendations

analyze_mcp_costs.description: Rank MCPs by cost - see which skills drain budget most

get_recommendations.description: Get cost optimization recommendations ranked by impact

detect_anomalies.description: Detect unusual spending patterns or spikes

build_custom_report.description: Build advanced custom reports with filtering and aggregation

create_organization.description: Create multi-org structure for team cost tracking

get_compliance_status.description: Check compliance status (SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI DSS, ISO 27001)

export_to_observability.description: Export metrics and traces to observability backends

trackPdfUrlRead.description: Track reading a PDF via URL (3.6x multiplier)
trackPdfUrlRead.code:
reporter.track_operation(
  operation_type='file_read',
  tokens_input=450,
  tokens_output=120,
  model='claude-3-5-haiku',
  file_source='pdf_url'  # 3.6x more expensive!
)

trackDatabaseQuery.code:
reporter.track_operation(
  operation_type='database_query',
  tokens_input=100000,  # Snowflake result set
  tokens_output=50000,
  model='claude-3-5-sonnet',
  session_id='data-exploration'
)

analyzeSession.description: Analyze a session to find where money is going
analyzeSession.code:
analysis = reporter.analyze_session('debug-auth')
print(f"Session cost: ${analysis['total_cost_usd']}")
print(f"Biggest waste: {analysis['biggest_waste']['type']}")
for rec in analysis['recommendations']:
  print(f"Save ${rec['cost_usd']}: {rec['suggestion']}")

installation.source: git clone https://github.com/Mullassery/PyCostAudit.git && cd PyCostAudit && make install
